channel.title: Blog on gameandcode
channel.description: Recent content in Blog on gameandcode

item.title: GameDev mind map
item.description: Special announcement I am pleased to announce that a Discord server now exists. I would be happy to meet a few people. You can expect the latest game dev news and resources. Get feedback for your projects or just talk about games. I will also post news about my upcoming games. You will also be one of the first to get the chance to test them. Introduction This post will give you a quick overview about the game dev mind map I created to organize useful web links.

item.title: Devs That Jam #3
item.description: Introduction At the end of June 2024 I participated in another game jam. This time it was Devs That Jam, which is a 36 hour event. We actually won the jam! If you are not familiar with game jams, here is a brief description: Game Jams are creative events where individuals or teams come together to design and develop a complete game in a limited timeframe. These events emphasize collaboration, innovation, and rapid prototyping, and often revolve around a specific theme or challenge.

item.title: One of those "I quit my job" posts
item.description: Introduction I recently decided to take game development more seriously. This blog post describes my first steps to becoming self-employed. Why am I doing this? I’ve been thinking about starting my own business for a long time. Especially on long evenings when I worked late into the night on games. Now I want to devote my full concentration to game development. Of course, personal freedom and other things that come with being self-employed are also important to me.

item.title: Advent calendar template
item.description: Your browser does not support the video tag. Advent calendar preview Introduction Hello fellow game developers! This blog post will explain how to use an advent calendar template I created in unity. You can change the complete look of the calendar and create custom content for each day. All the assets I used are free (with attribution) and can be used by you. The resource code is available on github or as a Unity package in the code section.

item.title: Random position spawning
item.description: Table of Contents Introduction Random position offscreen Random position on screen And now you! Introduction One of the key elements that contributes to the interactivity of a game is the ability to seamlessly spawn objects into the game world. Whether it’s an enemy unit stepping out of the shadows, power-ups magically appearing, or obstacles falling from the sky, the art of spawning objects is a fundamental skill for game developers.

item.title: Kenney Jam 2023 recap
item.description: Table of Contents Introduction Game concept First day (Basic implementation) Second day (Look and feel) Third day (Itch.io page and polish) Result Lessons learned And now you! Screenshots Bibliography Introduction Programming a game over a long period of time can be very exhausting. Many indie game developers give up their games during the development process. One reason might be, that the games are too big or too complex to be handled solo.
